q1.prompt: Which first-generation cephalosporin is used as a standard surgical prophylaxis to prevent *Staphylococcus aureus* wound infections by inhibiting peptidoglycan cross-linking?

q2.prompt: A 28-year-old male presents with a single, painless ulcer on his penis that appeared one week ago. Physical examination reveals a clean-based, indurated ulcer and bilateral, non-tender inguinal lymphadenopathy. Darkfield microscopic examination of the lesion exudate demonstrates motile, tightly coiled spirochetes. Which of the following is the most appropriate treatment of choice for this patient?

q3.prompt: A 6-year-old child is diagnosed with a tick-borne infection. Which of the following antibiotic classes should be avoided in this patient due to the risk of permanent tooth discoloration and inhibition of bone growth?

q4.prompt: Which bactericidal agent requires an oxygen-dependent transporter to enter the cell and exerts its effect by binding irreversibly to the 30S ribosomal subunit, causing mRNA misreading?

q5.prompt: A 65-year-old patient presents with a productive cough, and sputum Gram stain reveals Gram-positive, lancet-shaped diplococci. Which of the following is the most appropriate empirical treatment if high-level penicillin resistance via altered penicillin-binding proteins is suspected?

q6.prompt: A patient presents with a few hairless, hypopigmented skin plaques on his trunk that have a complete loss of sensation. Physical examination also reveals visible enlargement of the ulnar and peroneal nerves. A skin biopsy confirms the presence of acid-fast bacilli. He received the appropriate treatment for this condition. Ten days later, he presents with significant fatigue, jaundice, and dark-coloured urine. Laboratory evaluation reveals evidence of acute hemolytic anemia. Which of the following drugs is most likely to be the cause of this patient's new symptoms?

q7.prompt: A patient being treated for a vaginal infection develops severe flushing, tachycardia, and hypotension after consuming a glass of wine. Which of the following drugs is most likely responsible for this reaction?

q8.prompt: Which of the following describes the molecular mechanism by which ciprofloxacin inhibits bacterial DNA synthesis?

options.label: Binding to the 30S ribosomal subunit to prevent initiation
Inhibition of the beta-subunit of RNA polymerase
Inhibition of DNA gyrase and topoisomerase IV

q9.prompt: A patient with cystic fibrosis develops a pulmonary infection with *Pseudomonas aeruginosa*. Which of the following drugs treats this infection by disrupting bacterial cell wall synthesis?

q10.prompt: For which patient population or clinical scenario is the use of bacteriostatic agents generally discouraged in favor of bactericidal therapy?

options.label: Young, sexually active women with simple cystitis
A patient with a secondary pharyngitis during infectious mononucleosis
Pediatric patients with minor respiratory tract infections
An immunocompromised host or a patient with a life-threatening infection
A patient with a localized, uncomplicated skin infection
